prompt: Create a new Rust binary crate at status/visual-tests/ for visual regression testing. Cargo.toml should depend on: image = 0.25, pixelmatch (or implement a simple pixel comparison fn). The binary should: 1) Take two PNG paths as args (expected vs actual). 2) Compare pixel-by-pixel at zero tolerance. 3) Output a diff PNG highlighting mismatches in red. 4) Exit 0 if identical, exit 1 if different, printing mismatch count. Write src/main.rs. Make it compile with cargo check.

Create src/domains/ui/screenshot.rs — a Bevy system for headless screenshot capture. It should: 1) Define a ScreenshotRequest resource with output_path: String and frames_to_wait: u32. 2) Define a capture_screenshot system that runs in PostUpdate, decrements frames_to_wait, and when it hits 0 captures the screen to output_path using bevy render screenshot API. 3) Define a ScreenshotPlugin that adds the resource and system. 4) Add mod screenshot to the existing ui module. Write the file but do NOT modify other files. Include usage comments.

Create src/bin/asset_catalogue.rs — a standalone Bevy binary that renders game assets one by one for visual regression. It should: 1) Define an enum CatalogueState with variants for each asset category (EnemySprites, DjinnSprites, UIElements, WorldTiles). 2) Use Bevy States to cycle through categories. 3) For each state, spawn placeholder colored rectangles (since no real sprites exist yet) at known positions with labels. 4) After N frames per state, capture a screenshot to screenshots/<state_name>.png. 5) After all states rendered, exit via AppExit. This is the vertical-slice renderer. Make it compile standalone.

Write scripts/visual-regression.sh — a bash script for CI that: 1) Runs the asset catalogue binary with headless rendering (WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan or software). 2) Compares each output screenshot against screenshots/expected/ using the visual-test binary. 3) Reports pass/fail per screenshot. 4) If any fail, generates a diff image in screenshots/diffs/. 5) Exits 0 only if all pass. Include comments about SwiftShader/Lavapipe setup.

Write a reg-suit configuration at status/visual-ci-config.json suitable for GitHub Actions integration. Include: image directory paths (screenshots/actual vs screenshots/expected), threshold 0 for pixel-perfect, HTML report output. Also write a draft GitHub Actions workflow at status/visual-ci-workflow.yml that runs on PR: checkout, install deps (mesa-vulkan-drivers, xvfb), cargo build the catalogue binary, xvfb-run it, then run the comparison script.
